fields.title: Squat Reach
fields.description: The Squat to Reach is a dynamic warm-up exercise that combines a deep squat with an overhead reach.

fields.title: T Plank Excercise
fields.description: The T Plank is a dynamic core-strengthening exercise that combines the traditional plank with a rotational movement to form a "T" shape.

fields.title: Stability Ball Wall Squat
fields.description: This lower body exercise uses a large stability (Swiss) ball placed between your lower back and a wall. As you squat down, the ball rolls with your back.

fields.title: Squat
fields.description: The Squat to Reach is a dynamic warm-up exercise that combines a deep squat with an overhead reach.

fields.title: Russian Twist
fields.description: A core exercise performed sitting with legs lifted, twisting the torso side to side to engage the obliques and abdominal muscles.

fields.title: Leg Raises
fields.description: A core exercise where you lie on your back and lift your legs upward, focusing on the lower abdominal muscles.

fields.title: Push up
fields.description: A bodyweight exercise where you lower and raise your body using your arms while keeping your body straight, targeting the chest, shoulders, and triceps.

fields.title: Pull up
fields.description: An upper-body exercise where you hang from a bar and pull your chin above it, primarily working the back, biceps, and shoulders.

fields.title: Deadlift
fields.description: A compound weightlifting move where you lift a loaded barbell from the floor to hip level, targeting the lower back, glutes, hamstrings, and core

fields.title: Bench Press
fields.description: A strength exercise where you lie on a bench and press a barbell upward, mainly working the chest, shoulders, and triceps.

fields.title: Tricep Pushdown
fields.description: An isolation exercise using a cable machine to push a handle down with your arms, targeting the triceps.

fields.title: Lateral Raise
fields.description: A shoulder exercise where you lift dumbbells or weights out to the sides, focusing on the lateral deltoid muscles
